operating_state: Revenue increased 1% to $28.5 billion driven by diabetes franchise expansion with Mounjaro launch and Trulicity growth, alongside Jardiance collaboration revenue increases. Oncology revenue declined due to Alimta patent expiry and generic competition. COVID-19 antibody treatments contributed revenue but lost FDA emergency use authorization as pandemic conditions evolved.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 2.6 percentage points to 76.8% reflecting favorable product mix toward higher-margin biologics and absence of prior year inventory impairment charges, partially offset by pricing pressures and inflation. Research and development expenses increased 4% driven by late-stage pipeline development with 45 candidates across therapeutic areas. Marketing and administrative expenses remained flat despite inflationary pressures.
material_events: Mounjaro received FDA approval for diabetes treatment with compound patent protection through 2036 and rapid market uptake. Completed Akouos acquisition for gene therapy capabilities expansion. Repurchased $1.5 billion in shares and increased quarterly dividend. Committed over $4 billion to new manufacturing facilities for incretin capacity expansion. Settled Taltz patent litigation with Novartis through cashless mutual release agreement.
macro_exposures: Inflation Reduction Act implementation beginning 2023 introduces government price-setting for Medicare drugs with first negotiated prices effective 2026. Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenue by 3% with continued exposure through foreign currency-denominated debt. Supply chain inflation and logistics strain from pandemic and geopolitical tensions increased manufacturing costs. European cost-cutting reforms activated clawback mechanisms and increased mandatory rebates.
forward_outlook: Anticipates tight incretin product supplies until additional manufacturing capacity becomes operational over next several years with significant expansion in 2023. Late-stage pipeline includes donanemab for Alzheimer's disease under FDA review, lebrikizumab for atopic dermatitis submitted globally, and mirikizumab for ulcerative colitis under regulatory review. Management expects GLP-1 products to represent significant and growing portion of business.
uncertainty_profile: Patent challenges through Paragraph IV certifications create ongoing exclusivity risks across product portfolio with generic competition pressure. Tax Cuts and Jobs Act R&D capitalization requirement increased cash tax payments with continued elevated payments over five-year amortization period. Multiple ongoing legal proceedings include insulin pricing litigation and 340B program disputes. Alzheimer's coverage determination limits Medicare coverage to clinical trial participants, potentially constraining donanemab commercial access.
